text.title: methodologies Core Rules
text.summary: Four testable rules for using this dispatcher as routing, not manual.

rule.statement: Always hop to the sibling AGENTS.md. This index is a map, not a manual. Load the sibling AGENTS.md before implementing anything.
rule.rationale: Dispatcher carries names only; siblings carry the authoritative rules.

rule.statement: Validate slug names against the actual filesystem before delegating. Never use names from historical rows in this index.
rule.rationale: Slugs drift; verification prevents downstream 404 loads.

rule.statement: Return exactly one slug per dispatch (or zero with escalation). Never multi-pick — that's the catalogue's job.
rule.rationale: Dispatcher pre-routes to one slug; downstream uses files-reference for multi-candidate selection.

rule.statement: Never invent a slug. If no keyword matches, return empty candidates and escalate to semantic Grep.
Match found, slug resolved on disk, sibling AGENTS.md loaded before action.
rule.rationale: Aggregate conclusion for the positive branch.

rule.statement: No keyword match — defer to files-reference catalogue or semantic Grep.
rule.rationale: Aggregate conclusion for the negative branch.
